hypothesis: Replacing the default tokenizer with a chemistry-aware tokenizer that merges frequent MOFid substrings (metal symbols, linker SMILES motifs, and RCSR topology codes) during continual pre-training, while holding architecture, corpora, and compute constant, will reduce QMOF band-gap MAE by at least 10% relative to the PMC+PubChem model when fine-tuned with at most 1K SFT examples.
